published_prior_art.cite: BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 and RothSantAnna2023Efficient establish efficiency/dominance benchmarks, not a computable rank-gap loss formula for SA/CS/W relative to BJS under common parallel-trends aggregation

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Pi_H projects onto row(H) subset R^m, but the statement writes (I-Pi_H) Delta_tau; dimensions require right projection Delta_tau (I-Pi_H)=0, matching row(Delta_tau) subset H.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The variance-gap norm repeats the same left-projection expression ||(I-Pi_H) Delta_tau|| even though Delta_tau is q x m and Pi_H is m x m; the displayed formula is dimensionally undefined until rewritten with right projection.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite every active-row-space residual as Delta_tau (I-Pi_H), update the abstract, Introduction, Conjectures 1-2, and the reported diagnostic norm consistently, and state that rows of Delta_tau are projected in R^m.

Add one sentence in the BJS comparison acknowledging their restricted Wooldridge/imputation equivalence result, then state precisely why the proposed iff frontier strictly extends beyond that restricted class.
Pin the dimensions of each Z_M explicitly after vectorization so P_M is a projection on R^{qm}; this will make rho_M mechanically checkable and avoid another well-posedness objection in Stage 0.

recommended_next_step: Patch the projection orientation and Z_M dimension conventions, then rerun Stage -0.5; the novelty kernel otherwise clears the flagship floor.
